claim.statement: The BDBV Stage Two R prior gamma(alpha=4.0, beta=3.0), mean about 1.33, is grounded in BDBV-specific R0 estimates.

sources.citation: Wamala JF, et al. Emerging Infectious Diseases 2010; 16(7):1087-1092. DOI 10.3201/eid1607.091525.
sources.finding: Supports BDBV case count, CFR, incubation, and inter-case interval. Does not report R0.

sources.citation: Van Kerkhove MD, et al. Scientific Data 2015;2:150019. DOI 10.1038/sdata.2015.19.
sources.finding: Review states: 'R0 has not been estimated for Ebola Bundibugyo.'

sources.citation: Lekone PE, Finkenstadt BF. Biometrics 2006;62(4):1170-1177. DOI 10.1111/j.1541-0420.2006.00609.x.
sources.finding: Stochastic SEIR framework reference only. It predates BDBV discovery and does not ground a 2007 Bundibugyo R0 estimate.

steps.finding: Wamala 2010 contains the outbreak shape used by the prior: 116 cases, CFR 34%, median incubation 7 days, and 3 successive cycles. No R0 parameter is reported.
The 2015 Scientific Data parameter review explicitly says BDBV R0 had not been estimated. This contradicts treating the gamma prior as BDBV-specific literature-grounded.
The parameterization is internally coherent: mean = 4.0 / 3.0 = 1.33 and standard deviation = sqrt(4.0) / 3.0 = 0.67.
No direct BDBV R0 source was found. Keep the value only as a clearly labeled interim modeling prior, or rederive from a verified source before claiming primary-source grounding.

chains.next_action: Do not cite gamma(4.0, 3.0) as BDBV-specific R0 evidence until a direct source or explicit derivation is added.

claim.statement: Module C reporting-completeness interval is derived from a single peer-reviewed onset-to-notification gamma cited from Camacho 2015, applied cross-species from EBOV-Zaire to BDBV as best available proxy.
claim.value: gamma(0.81, 0.18) shape-rate, mean 4.5 d, s.d. 5 d

sources.citation: Camacho A, et al. PLOS Currents Outbreaks 2015. DOI 10.1371/currents.outbreaks.406ae55e83ec0b5193e30856b9235ed2.
sources.finding: Appendix 1 reports the empirical EBOV-Zaire onset-to-notification distribution as over-dispersed with mean 4.5 d, s.d. 5 d. Methods state Camacho's own dynamic model uses an over-dispersed delay with average 4.3 d. The 4.5 d / 5 d s.d. parameters ground the corrected TOTAL_DELAY_GAMMA via method-of-moments.

sources.citation: Cori A, et al. American Journal of Epidemiology 2013;178(9):1505-1512. DOI 10.1093/aje/kwt133.
sources.finding: EpiEstim methodology paper. Does not source any presentation-to-report delay distribution. Removed from PRIOR_CITATIONS as part of the correction.

sources.citation: WHO Ebola Response Team. New England Journal of Medicine 2014;371:1481-1495. DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa1411100.
sources.finding: Reports incubation 11.4 d and serial interval 15.3 d in the accessible abstract. Full text was 403 paywalled. Does not source a report-to-confirmation 3-day delay. Removed from PRIOR_CITATIONS as part of the correction.

steps.finding: Initial audit found the 34-41 percent output internally derived from three gammas summing by method-of-moments to gamma(7.5, 1.0), but the three stage-specific source attributions did not support the stated gamma parameters.

Pre-correction: gamma_cdf(7, 7.5, 1.0) is approximately 0.4745; Beta-Binomial post mean is 53/657 which is approximately 0.081; mixed 3 to 1 yields 0.376, matching the displayed [0.340, 0.412] interval. Post-correction: gamma_cdf(7, 0.81, 0.18) is approximately 0.786; mixed 3 to 1 with 0.081 yields 0.610, matching the new [0.591, 0.626] interval.

Collapsed the three-stage chain to a single TOTAL_DELAY_GAMMA = (0.81, 0.18), method-of-moments matched to Camacho 2015 Appendix 1 (mean 4.5 d, s.d. 5 d). Removed ONSET_TO_PRESENTATION_GAMMA, PRESENTATION_TO_REPORT_GAMMA, REPORT_TO_CONFIRMATION_GAMMA. Trimmed PRIOR_CITATIONS to Camacho alone with an explicit EBOV-Zaire to BDBV cross-species acknowledgment. Reduced alpha_sigma from 0.5 to 0.10 and the sampled-alpha floor from 0.5 to 0.1 to keep relative shape-parameter uncertainty consistent with the new alpha. Bumped MODEL_VERSION to lovs_visibility-v0.2.0. Re-ran refresh_pipeline.py and sync_to_website.py: snapshot now shows visibility.grade low, reportingCompleteness50 [0.591, 0.626], publicationLatencyDays50 [1.1, 6.6].
2026-05-21 recheck against the open PDF confirmed the exact Appendix statement: onset-to-notification mean 4.5 days and s.d. 5 days. Method-of-moments gamma(0.81, 0.18) remains source-backed.

chains.next_action: Keep Module C delay prior linked to this chain. Follow-ups: re-derive any website prose or brief copy referencing 34-41 percent or very_low; revisit Camacho 2015 ref [10] to deepen citation; evaluate Module D corridor-risk visibility-adjustment factor change from approximately 2.95x to approximately 1.69x.

claim.statement: The BDBV prior-outbreak CFR scenario band is 26/33/40 percent (central 33), per the US CDC 55/169 aggregate and the Imperial 20 May 2026 update. It supersedes the 18 May 24/30/40 set and the older unsupported 30-50 percent range.

sources.citation: Wamala JF, et al. Emerging Infectious Diseases 2010;16(7):1087-1092. DOI 10.3201/eid1607.091525.
sources.finding: Reports the 2007-2008 Uganda BDBV outbreak CFR as 34 percent (39/116 = 33.6).

sources.citation: US CDC. History of Ebola Outbreaks.
sources.finding: Aggregates prior BDBV outbreaks as 55 deaths and 169 cases (32.5 percent central).

sources.citation: Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the BDBV outbreak in DRC: 20 May 2026 update.
sources.finding: Corrects the 18 May 24/30/40 percent CFR set to 26/33/40, with central 32.5 percent (55/169) and the 26-40 band as the Wilson 95% CI; deaths input updated from 88 to 131.

sources.citation: Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in DRC. 18 May 2026 (superseded).
sources.finding: The superseded 18 May report used 24, 30, and 40 percent CFR scenarios; its 30 percent central did not match the 55/169 = 32.5 percent aggregate.

steps.finding: Wamala supports the point estimate 39 / 116 = 33.6 percent, not a 30-50 percent range.
CDC aggregate 55 / 169 = 32.5 percent central (rounds to 33). Wilson 95% CI of 55/169 is [25.9 percent, 39.9 percent], which rounds to the 26 / 40 percent scenario bounds.

2026-05-21: superseded the 18 May 24/30/40 set with the Imperial 20 May update 26/33/40 (central 33). Updated lovs/lovs_death_back_projection.py (IMPERIAL_CFR_SCENARIOS, CENTRAL_CFR) and reconciled README.md, CITATIONS.md, NUMBERS_AUDIT.md, make_brief.py, and sync_to_website.py. The website InferredTrajectory.tsx / methodology_constants surface updates on the next sync_to_website.py run.

chains.next_action: Keep the CFR band linked to this chain at 26/33/40 (central 33). Re-sync the website snapshot's methodology_constants so the live InferredTrajectory band reflects 26/40. Avoid reintroducing the 24/30/40 set or the unsupported 30-50 percent range.

claim.statement: The BDBV Stage Two detection-depth priors are source-backed where possible: serial interval is bracketed by Wamala 2010, incubation is anchored by MacNeil 2010 and Wamala 2010, Albariño 2013 supports same-species consistency only, and under-ascertainment remains an explicit heuristic range.

sources.citation: Wamala JF, et al. Emerging Infectious Diseases 2010;16(7):1087-1092. DOI 10.3201/eid1607.091525.
sources.finding: Reports median incubation 7 days (range 2-20) and three successive transmission cycles with 3-11 day intervals.

sources.citation: MacNeil A, et al. Emerging Infectious Diseases 2010;16(12):1969-1972. DOI 10.3201/eid1612.100627.
sources.finding: Reports mean incubation 6.3 days among laboratory-confirmed cases (n=24), with survivors 5.7 days and fatal cases 7.4 days.

sources.citation: Albariño CG, et al. Virology 2013;442(2):97-100. DOI 10.1016/j.virol.2013.05.001.
sources.finding: Confirms the 2012 DRC outbreak as Bundibugyo virus emergence in DRC by genomic analysis. It does not report serial interval, incubation, or ascertainment parameters.

steps.finding: Wamala supports a BDBV inter-case interval bracket of 3-11 days and median incubation 7 days.
MacNeil supports the incubation anchor: mean 6.3 days in 24 laboratory-confirmed patients.
Albariño is valid only as a same-species/genomic consistency check for the 2012 DRC BDBV cluster. It must not be used as evidence for numeric transmission-delay parameters.

serial gamma(4.0, 0.55) has mean 7.27 d and s.d. 3.64 d, bracketing Wamala's 3-11 d interval. incubation gamma(4.0, 0.6) has mean 6.67 d and s.d. 3.33 d, consistent with MacNeil mean 6.3 d and Wamala range 2-20 d.
under_ascertainment_uniform(0.3, 0.9) is retained as a heuristic early-outbreak surveillance range, not as a BDBV-specific primary-source parameter.

chains.next_action: Keep serial and incubation priors source-linked; do not describe Albariño or the under-ascertainment uniform as direct numeric grounding.

claim.statement: The Module D corridor gravity exponents, normalizers, and clamp are literature-grounded parameter values.
claim.value: log-population product / 100, road product / 25, healthcare log distance / 5, conflict multiplier, clamp [0.1, 10.0]

sources.citation: Backer JA, Wallinga J. PLOS Computational Biology 2016;12(12):e1005210. DOI 10.1371/journal.pcbi.1005210.
sources.finding: Supports the family of gravity-type spatial-dispersion models for WA 2014 Ebola and reports district-level spatiotemporal data, but does not support LOVS's exact covariate exponents or clamps.

sources.citation: Glynn JR, et al. Journal of Infectious Diseases 2018;217(2):232-237. DOI 10.1093/infdis/jix579.
sources.finding: Supports household secondary attack rate magnitude (18% in the open PDF), not a cross-prefecture per-case hazard coefficient or gravity exponent set.

sources.citation: Faye O, et al. Lancet Infectious Diseases 2015;15(3):320-326. DOI 10.1016/S1473-3099(14)71075-8.
sources.finding: Supports setting-specific reproduction estimates in Conakry chains, not LOVS corridor exponents or the 100x household-to-cross-prefecture scaling.

sources.citation: Camacho A, et al. PLOS Currents Outbreaks 2015. DOI 10.1371/currents.outbreaks.406ae55e83ec0b5193e30856b9235ed2.
sources.finding: Supports a stochastic SEIR/reporting-delay framework for Sierra Leone, not LOVS corridor gravity exponents.

steps.finding: Backer and Wallinga support the general gravity-type spatial-dispersion family using distance and population size, so the broad modeling family is defensible.
Glynn supports household SAR magnitude, but LOVS's per-case cross-prefecture coefficient 0.003 and the approximately 100x down-scaling are heuristic.
The edge-weight formula is internally deterministic and bounded, but the constants /100, /25, /5, target conflict multiplier, and [0.1, 10.0] clamp are not fitted or quoted from any cited source.
No source-backed exponent fit exists in the repo. Treat Module D corridor exponents as transparent engineering heuristics until fitted against a validated mobility/spatial-spread dataset.

chains.next_action: Do not present the current corridor exponents as literature-grounded. Fit or calibrate them before claiming source-backed corridor discrimination.

claim.statement: The WA 2014 retrospective skill scores are computed from a Backer-Wallinga 62-prefecture, 74-week substrate; the reported W3-W11 evaluation window is a local deterministic protocol, not a source-imposed capture range.
claim.value: W3, W5, W7, W9, W11 as-of weeks; 4-week horizon; no-context Brier 0.0586, WIS 0.1002, ECE 0.0391

sources.citation: Backer JA, Wallinga J. PLOS Computational Biology 2016;12(12):e1005210. DOI 10.1371/journal.pcbi.1005210.
sources.finding: Supports the WA 2014 district-level Ebola spatiotemporal substrate and says relevant data are in the supporting information.

sources.citation: data/west-africa-prefecture-weekly.json metadata, derived from Backer-Wallinga S1 Data.
sources.finding: Local substrate contains 62 prefectures, 74 weekly counts per prefecture, source SHA-256 cc2ae026ec7d2ce73977a05d727b14db93d0dc110b4455c2d7b88a7879ece663, and declaration-week alignment rationale.

sources.citation: lovs/lovs_validation.py deterministic Mode A backtest.
sources.finding: Defines as_of_weeks=(3,5,7,9,11), horizon_weeks=4, and computes deterministic Brier/WIS/ECE scores.

steps.finding: Backer-Wallinga supports use of weekly district-level WA 2014 incidence through June 2015; the local archive concretizes this as 62 prefectures by 74 weeks.
Local JSON shape check: 62 prefectures, all with 74 weekly counts; 56 prefectures ever active.
The pending-queue phrase '1 to 11 week capture range' was imprecise. The code and reproduced output use W3, W5, W7, W9, W11 as-of dates, not W1-W11.

chains.next_action: Keep the W3-W11 window described as a local validation protocol; cite Backer-Wallinga only for the substrate, not for the window choice.

claim.artifact: not redistributed: restricted local review notes for Imperial Table 3
claim.locator: Nord Kivu and Ituri+Nord Kivu Table 3 row-sum check
claim.statement: Imperial Table 3's Nord Kivu summary total can be adjudicated against the underlying WHO epiweek 10/11/15/18 sitreps.
claim.value: Imperial's published Nord Kivu PoE summary total does not reconcile with the sum of its own Table 3 per-PoE rows (off by 18 daily travellers); absolute counts are restricted and not redistributed here

sources.citation: Imperial College MRC GIDA and WHO. Estimation of the size of the outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in DRC. 18 May 2026.
sources.finding: Local PDF Table 3 gives per-PoE rows and summary totals. It cites WHO-shared PoE data, not the underlying raw sitreps.

sources.citation: WHO AFRO Weekly External Situation Report 01, data as of 18 May 2026.
sources.finding: Situation report landing page and downloadable PDF are accessible, but the PDF does not contain the epiweek 10/11/15/18 point-of-entry passenger table needed to adjudicate Imperial Table 3.

sources.citation: Restricted local review notes for Imperial Table 3; not redistributed in the public repository.
sources.finding: The public repository no longer redistributes the extracted PoE table. Only the discrepancy summary and source attribution remain in the evidence chain.

steps.finding: The Ituri per-PoE rows reconcile to Imperial's declared Ituri total. The Nord Kivu per-PoE rows sum to 18 daily travellers above Imperial's declared Nord Kivu total, and the combined total inherits the same 18-traveller discrepancy. The individual per-PoE passenger counts are restricted Imperial Table 3 data and are not redistributed here.
Imperial Table 3 states the PoE data came from four one-week sitreps covering epiweeks 10, 11, 15, and 18, shared by WHO on 16 May 2026.

The public WHO AFRO sitrep 01 PDF was retrievable on 2026-05-21 (SHA-256 b43fc698bf2407323c6d6af25a3679eccdd2d68b3effc61a7563e6ef977366ba), but text extraction found only general PoE preparedness language, not the passenger-count table.
The underlying WHO epiweek passenger-count sitreps are still not in the repo or public WHO sitrep 01 PDF, so the discrepancy cannot be adjudicated beyond row-sum correction.

chains.next_action: Do not redistribute the extracted Table 3 PoE dataset. Reopen only if WHO publishes the underlying epiweek 10/11/15/18 PoE passenger-count sitreps, Imperial grants permission, or Imperial issues an erratum.

claim.locator: entries[].license for WHO-published sources (who-don602, who-pheic, afro-sitrep)
claim.statement: WHO Disease Outbreak News and situation-report content archived in this repo is licensed CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO.

sources.citation: World Health Organization. Copyright and permissions policy. who.int/about/policies/publishing/copyright.
sources.finding: States that publications published by WHO are licensed under CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO. The more permissive CC BY 3.0 IGO and CC BY-NC 3.0 IGO apply only to WHO-staff or WHO-funded articles in non-WHO publications, not to WHO's own Disease Outbreak News.

sources.citation: WHO Disease Outbreak News item 2026-DON602, archived 2026-05-20 (SHA-256 8b7fb1e1c8403b7a6015c804a3cd818c04b649ca23d791fe957e59119818218f).
sources.finding: The DON page footer shows a bare copyright line ('(c) 2026 WHO') and a Terms-of-use link rather than an inline CC badge; the applicable license is WHO's standard publications license CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO per the copyright policy.

steps.finding: WHO copyright policy: publications published by WHO are CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO (attribution, non-commercial, share-alike).

The manifest, the WHO_DON_602_TARGET ingest target in lovs/lovs_live_ingest.py, and the test fixtures previously recorded CC-BY-3.0-IGO for WHO sources. That is WHO's permissive staff-article license, not the publications license, and it understated WHO's reserved NonCommercial and ShareAlike rights.

Changed every WHO-publisher license field from CC-BY-3.0-IGO to CC-BY-NC-SA-3.0-IGO: data/bundibugyo-2026/manifest.json (3 entries), lovs/lovs_live_ingest.py WHO_DON_602_TARGET, tests/data/lovs/fixture/manifest.json (5 entries), tests/test_lovs_live_ingest.py. Prose in README.md, CITATIONS.md, make_brief.py, LICENSES.md, NOTICE, and the website footer already used CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO and is now consistent across machine and human fields.

chains.next_action: Reconciled. Reopen only if WHO publishes a page-level CC notice for Disease Outbreak News that differs from the standard CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO publications license.

claim.statement: The archived Imperial College MRC GIDA 18 May 2026 report is recorded under a restricted-use CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 assumption pending the primary license footer.

sources.citation: Archived Imperial MRC GIDA report landing page, raw/647610bf3450407ec3313e2a17642b0ee9cbec7f14c72665318993f06acaf8b4.
sources.finding: The archived bytes are the report landing-page HTML. Footer shows only a bare copyright ('(c) Imperial College London'); there is no inline Creative Commons notice. The report PDF that would carry the license footer is not byte-archived in this repo.

sources.citation: Imperial College MRC Centre for Global Infectious Disease Analysis, main site, checked 2026-05-20.
sources.finding: Site footer shows only '(c) 2026 Imperial College London' with no site-level Creative Commons license; per-report license terms live in each report PDF, which is not held here.

steps.finding: Grep of the archived landing-page bytes returns a generic copyright mention and no Creative Commons / by-nc-nd string.

The live MRC GIDA site likewise exposes no site-level CC license. Repo prose and the optional PoE helper consistently record CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 from the report's stated terms at access time; extracted Table 3 data is no longer redistributed in the public tree.

Replaced the non-standard manifest label 'imperial-college-research-output' with 'CC-BY-NC-ND-4.0' so the machine field matches the repo-wide restricted-use assumption. Added a manifest license_note documenting the confirmation gap. The repository should treat Imperial-derived table data and archived publisher bytes as restricted third-party material until the primary license footer or permission is archived.

The authoritative license footer is in the report PDF, which is not byte-archived; the landing page and live site do not restate the CC variant. The exact license string therefore cannot be re-confirmed from repo-held bytes.

chains.next_action: Byte-archive the Imperial report PDF (or its license footer) to confirm CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 directly. Until then the value is the conservative assumption, not a byte-verified quote.

claim.statement: The May 2026 BDBV staged count observations preserve separate confirmed, probable, suspected, suspected-death, and approximate-media denominators instead of treating CDC, WHO speech, ECDC, and aggregator values as interchangeable.
claim.value: CDC 2026-05-20/data-as-of 2026-05-19 exact tuple; WHO DG 2026-05-20 confirmed endpoint and approximate suspected burden; aggregator values cross-check only.

sources.citation: US CDC. Ebola Disease: Current Situation. 20 May 2026.
sources.finding: Reports, as of 19 May, 536 suspected cases, 105 probable cases, 34 confirmed cases, and 134 suspected deaths; also reports 11 DRC health zones in Ituri and Nord-Kivu as of 20 May.

sources.citation: WHO Director-General's opening remarks at the media briefing on Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda, 20 May 2026.
sources.finding: Reports 51 confirmed DRC cases, two confirmed Uganda cases, almost 600 suspected cases, and 139 suspected deaths.

sources.citation: WHO Disease Outbreak News DON602, Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, DRC and Uganda, 16 May 2026.
sources.finding: Provides the May 15 baseline: 246 suspected cases and 80 deaths in DRC, Bundibugyo confirmation, and initial affected health zones.

sources.citation: ECDC. Ebola virus disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda, 19 May 2026.
sources.finding: Reports 30 laboratory-confirmed cases, over 500 suspected cases, and 130 deaths; useful as a regional cross-check rather than a replacement for CDC/WHO denominators.

steps.finding: CDC is the cleanest structured official tuple for data as of 19 May and must be staged as four distinct denominators: suspected, probable, confirmed, and suspected deaths.
WHO's 20 May remarks provide a later confirmed endpoint and approximate suspected burden; the 'almost 600' phrasing must not be coerced into an exact numeric input.
The staged May 19 tuple 536/105/34/134 had been attributed to ECDC, but that exact tuple is from CDC Current Situation. The observed staging now uses source_id cdc-current-situation-2026-05-20.

chains.next_action: Keep denominator-specific staged observations. Do not release a May 21 snapshot until an official source with published_at 2026-05-21 is archived; if no such source appears, carry this watch into the May 22 release check.

claim.statement: The reported South Kivu/Bukavu BDBV signal is an AP/AFP-reported M23/AFC claim and is not confirmed by a public-health authority.
claim.value: watch only; not a model input

sources.citation: Associated Press. M23 reports Ebola case near Bukavu/South Kivu, 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Wire report says M23 claimed a confirmed fatal case near Bukavu; AP is not the public-health authority for confirmation.

sources.citation: AFP via eNCA. Deadly DR Congo Ebola outbreak spreads to M23-held South Kivu, 21 May 2026.
sources.finding: Reports an M23 spokesman claim, notes Congolese authorities had not commented, and leaves sample origin around Bukavu uncertain.

sources.citation: WHO Director-General's opening remarks at the media briefing on Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda, 20 May 2026.
sources.finding: Official WHO geography through 20 May names Ituri and North Kivu in DRC, not South Kivu.

steps.finding: AP establishes that a South Kivu/Bukavu claim was publicly reported, but attributes it to M23.
AFP independently reports the same claim chain and explicitly notes lack of Congolese authority comment.
The latest official WHO geography in the archived source set is Ituri and North Kivu, so South Kivu remains unconfirmed for model purposes.

chains.next_action: Keep the South Kivu/Bukavu item in watch_signals only. Promote to staged_observations and geography inputs only after DRC MoH/INRB, WHO, Africa CDC, or CDC confirms it.
